title: CalculatePortfolioVaR ignores portfolio weights

evidence.quote: The function accepts returns and weights, validates both non-empty, then calculates VaR from returns only.

reasoning: Changing weights has no effect on the returned VaR, so the public API cannot calculate a weighted portfolio VaR. This is an API contract mismatch because weights are an explicit parameter and are only used as a non-empty gate.
reproduction: Call CalculatePortfolioVaR with the same returns and two very different non-empty weight slices; the results are identical.
recommendation: Either compute weighted portfolio returns before passing them to VaRCalculator, or remove/rename the weights parameter if the function is only intended to calculate unweighted VaR.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: risk_analytics_test.go covers HistoricalVaR, ParametricVaR, MonteCarloVaR, returns, rolling VaR, beta, and correlation, but includes no test for CalculatePortfolioVaR or for weight sensitivity.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a CalculatePortfolioVaR test where two different weight sets produce different weighted portfolio return series and therefore different VaR results.
